name: Osso Buco
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the flour with salt and pepper.
2. Dredge each veal shank piece in the seasoned flour and shake off excess.
3. Heat olive oil and butter in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
4. Quickly sear the veal on all sides, browning well. Transfer to paper towels to drain.
5. Add onions, garlic, basil, and oregano to the pan; cook over medium heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
6. Add tomatoes, salt, and pepper to taste; continue cooking for 10 minutes. Skim off any fat.
7. Stir in white wine and raise the heat. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes.
8. Return veal to the pan with just enough stock to cover the meat. Cover and let simmer for 1 to 1 1/2 hours, or bake at 350°F for the same time.
9. Remove lid and continue simmering or baking for an additional half hour until veal is tender.
10. Just before serving, sprinkle with lemon zest and parsley. Serve traditional with risotto Milanese. Try tossing small potatoes with olive oil and garlic or any pasta prepared similarly; share your thoughts!
